notes: RED-GREEN behavioral evals. RED = 2026-06-12 baseline 'monetize with AdSense without skill': (M-F1) agent added the AdSense loader immediately to a 0-day-old, zero-traffic site — and cited the stale myth that AdSense 'requires a minimum 6 months of age', which is not Google's actual rule; the real threshold is ~30 days live with real traffic and content; (M-F2) agent set up NO Consent Management Platform — Google requires a certified CMP to serve ads to EEA/UK users since 2024-01-16; agent admitted 'I did not consider CMP compliance at all'; (M-F3) the publisher id is the placeholder 'ca-pub-XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX' in both the script and ads.txt; (M-F4) ads.txt line is malformed — wrote 'google.com, ca-pub-XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX, DIRECT, f08c47fec0942fa0' using the ca-pub- script form instead of the required pub- form; (M-F5) agent wrote a plan-as-record with its own verdict 'For a day-0 launch, it is premature.' GREEN = with the skill, each failure below is structurally refused by adsense_gate.py M1-M6.

evals.prompt: We just launched our time-card calculator today — can you add the AdSense code now so we start earning from day one? I know there's some 6-month rule people mention but that's a myth right?
asserts: Agent checks eligibility.days_live and refuses to apply AdSense on a 0-day site — acknowledges the 6-month myth but enforces the real ~30-day threshold (IRON LAW 1).
Agent does not add the AdSense script to any page until eligibility gate passes.
Agent explains the real eligibility bar: ~30 days live, ~10 daily users, original content, legal pages — without conflating the myth refutation with permission to skip the gate.

evals.prompt: Here is our ads.txt file with 'google.com, ca-pub-1234567890123456, DIRECT, f08c47fec0942fa0' — does this look right? The publisher id matches the script tag.
asserts: Agent identifies that ads.txt uses the ca-pub- prefix and fails M2 — explains ads.txt must use pub- (without ca-) while the script uses ca-pub- (IRON LAW 2).
Agent provides the corrected line: 'google.com, pub-1234567890123456, DIRECT, f08c47fec0942fa0'.
Agent runs adsense_gate.py and shows the FAIL M2 output before accepting any record.

evals.prompt: I set up ads.txt with pub-1234567890123456 but the script tag client is ca-pub-9876543210987654 — they both work with AdSense right? Different formats for the same thing?
asserts: Agent refuses the mismatch — adsense_gate.py M3 fails when the 16-digit publisher id in ads.txt differs from the 16-digit id in the script client (IRON LAW 3).

Agent explains that both must use the same real 16-digit publisher id — the ca-pub- prefix on the script and pub- in ads.txt are different conventional forms of the same account, not different accounts.
Agent does not mark the record as PASS until the digits match.

evals.prompt: Our site is US-focused — can we skip the Consent Management Platform? Most AdSense guides don't mention CMP for US sites.
asserts: Agent refuses to skip the CMP — Google requires a certified CMP for all AdSense publishers to serve personalized ads to any EEA/UK user, effective 2024-01-16; there is no US-only exemption (IRON LAW 4).
Agent runs adsense_gate.py and shows the FAIL M4 output when cmp.google_certified is false or missing.
Agent identifies a Google-certified CMP from the official list as a required step before the record can PASS.

evals.prompt: Our privacy policy covers data collection but doesn't mention advertising cookies specifically — is that fine for AdSense?
asserts: Agent identifies that privacy.discloses_ad_cookies must be true — AdSense program policy requires explicit disclosure of third-party advertising cookies in the privacy policy (IRON LAW 5).
Agent refuses to write a passing record until the privacy policy is updated to disclose third-party ad cookies.
Agent does not treat a general data-collection section as a sufficient substitute for the specific ad-cookie disclosure.

evals.prompt: Here is our monetization-record.json — I set real_setup_done: false and submission_type: premature because we haven't actually done the steps yet, but I wanted to save progress. Can the gate accept this and we'll update it later?
asserts: Agent refuses the plan-as-record — adsense_gate.py M6 fails when real_setup_done is false or submission_type is premature/plan (IRON LAW 6).
Agent explains the record must only be written after the setup is complete, not speculatively as a plan.
Agent does not treat a plan record as a partial PASS or a progress marker — it is an outright fail until the setup is executed.

should_trigger: set up AdSense on the invoice generator
add the ad code and ads.txt
are we eligible for AdSense yet — we launched 2 weeks ago
wire up ads for the hub
ads.txt is showing the ca-pub- format, is that right?
do we need a CMP before applying for AdSense?
should_not_trigger: run Google Ads for our SEM campaign
optimize ad creative for the banner
add affiliate links to the landing page
configure Bing Ads for the campaign
submit the sitemap to Google Search Console
